conceptVectors.text: Risk: A business risk register entry describing a potential threat or hazard, its narrative description, and the owning business unit or entity it applies to.
Risk.sysId: Unique identifier / primary key of the risk record.
Risk.name: Short title or name of the risk.
Risk.description: Long-form narrative description of the risk and its potential impact.
Risk.profileSysId: Foreign key to the owning business unit, entity, department, or account this risk belongs to.
Risk.profileName: Display name of the owning business unit, entity, department, or account.
Control: A mitigating control, policy, or safeguard implemented to reduce the likelihood or impact of one or more risks, belonging to a control library or catalog.
Control.sysId: Unique identifier / primary key of the control record.
Control.name: Short title or name of the control.
Control.description: Narrative description of what the control does and how it mitigates risk.
Control.category: Category or classification of the control, e.g. Access Control, Database Security, Monitoring.
Control.profileSysId: Foreign key to the business unit, entity, or account that owns/implements this control.
Control.active: Boolean or status flag indicating whether the control is currently active/implemented.
TestEvidence: A control test or audit run and its result, capturing whether a control was verified effective, including linked test evidence and result notes.
TestEvidence.sysId: Unique identifier / primary key of the control test or test result record.
TestEvidence.number: Human-readable ticket/test number, e.g. TEST001.
TestEvidence.name: Short description or title of the test performed.
TestEvidence.state: Workflow state of the test, e.g. Complete, In Progress.
TestEvidence.effectiveness: Effectiveness rating of the control as determined by this test, e.g. Effective, Ineffective.
TestEvidence.status: Pass/fail status of the test result.
TestEvidence.latestResult: Free-text notes describing the outcome of the most recent test.
TestEvidence.resultDate: Date the test was performed or the result recorded.
TestEvidence.openIssues: List of unresolved issues or findings linked to this test or control.
TestEvidence.closedIssues: Count of resolved/closed issues linked to this test or control.
Issue: An audit finding, defect, or remediation issue raised against a control, test, risk, or entity, tracked through to closure.
Issue.sysId: Unique identifier / primary key of the issue record.
Issue.number: Human-readable issue/ticket number, e.g. ISS001.
Issue.desc: Short description of the finding or issue.
Issue.state: Workflow state of the issue, e.g. Open, In Progress, Closed Complete.
AssessmentInstance: A single run/instance of a risk assessment workflow linking a specific risk to its assessment lifecycle (inherent, control, residual stages).
AssessmentInstance.sysId: Unique identifier / primary key of the assessment instance record.
AssessmentInstance.riskSysId: Foreign key to the risk being assessed in this instance.

Factor: A scoreable rating factor within an assessment â€” either an inherent risk factor (e.g. data sensitivity, likelihood, impact) or a control-effectiveness factor â€” with a guidance rubric and a set of scoring choices.
Factor.sysId: Unique identifier / primary key of the factor response row within an assessment.
Factor.factorSysId: Foreign key to the factor definition / rating scale being answered.
Factor.factorName: Name of the factor, e.g. Data Sensitivity, Likelihood, Impact, Control Effectiveness.
Factor.factorDesc: Description of what this factor measures.
Factor.guidance: Rubric guidance text explaining how to select a rating for this factor.
Factor.choiceList: List of valid rating choice labels for this factor, e.g. High/Medium/Low or Satisfactory/Weak.
Factor.choiceMap: Mapping of each choice label to a numeric score.

goldStandardVectors.text: Risk table "sn_risk_risk": Enterprise risk register. One row per identified business risk with its narrative description and a link to the owning entity profile. Used as the root record that assessments and control mappings hang off.

Control table "sn_compliance_control": Controls library. One row per mitigating control or safeguard with description, category and active flag, scoped to an entity profile. Queried to build the candidate list when mapping controls to a risk.

AssessmentInstance table "sn_risk_advanced_risk_assessment_instance": Risk assessment header record. One row per assessment run of a specific risk, tracking its lifecycle state (inherent assessment, control assessment, residual, closed). The parent that factor response rows belong to. Used to find the current assessment and prior closed assessments of the same risk.

Factor table "sn_risk_advanced_risk_assessment_instance_response": Individual answer rows within an assessment instance. One row per factor to score â€” either linked to a specific control (control-effectiveness answers) or standalone (inherent factor answers). This is the table AI assessment results are written back into: score, rating comments and audit trail.

Factor table "sn_risk_advanced_factor": Factor definitions: what each rating dimension measures (data sensitivity, threat exposure, control effectiveness) plus the guidance rubric text an assessor follows when scoring it.

Factor table "sn_risk_advanced_factor_choice": Rating scale options for a factor: the valid choice labels (High/Medium/Low, Satisfactory/Weak) and the numeric score each maps to. Joined to factor definitions to build the answerable scale.

TestEvidence table "sn_audit_control_test": Control test executions. One row per audit test run against a control, holding workflow state, pass/fail status and the effectiveness verdict. Primary evidence source when judging whether a control actually works.

TestEvidence table "sn_audit_test_result": Detailed result notes for a control test: free-text outcome narrative and the testing date. Joined to the test record to enrich evidence with what was actually observed.

Issue table "sn_grc_issue": Findings and remediation issues raised against controls, tests, risks or entities, tracked by workflow state until Closed Complete. Open issues degrade control-effectiveness ratings and inform inherent risk scoring.
Control table "sn_risk_m2m_risk_control": Many-to-many junction linking risks to their mitigating controls. Rows are inserted here when the mapping agent decides a control addresses a risk.
Risk table "grc__Risk__c": Enterprise risk register object. One record per identified business risk, linked to a responsible business unit. Root object that risk assessments are created against.

Control table "grc__Control__c": Controls library object holding each mitigating control with description, category and implementation status, owned by a business unit. Queried as the candidate pool for risk-control mapping.

AssessmentInstance table "Risk__Risk_Assessment__c": Risk assessment header record. One row per assessment run of a specific risk with its assessment date. Parent of the inherent rating rows and the anchor for finding prior assessments of the same risk. Used by the inherent-assessment stage.

Factor table "Risk__Risk_Assessment_Rating__c": Inherent rating rows under an assessment: one row per scored dimension (Likelihood, Impact) with band, numeric value range and justification. This is where inherent-assessment AI results are written back.

Factor table "Risk__Control_Assessment__c": Control-effectiveness answer rows: one row per control being scored within a risk context, holding the effectiveness rating, numeric value and justification. This is where control-effectiveness AI results are written back. Used by the control-assessment stage.

Control table "Risk__Risk_Control_Lookup__c": Junction object linking a risk to a mitigating control, with the mapping justification. Rows are created here when the mapping agent selects controls for a risk.

TestEvidence table "grc__Control_Test_Result__c": Control test result records: pass/fail outcome, date of test and tester comments for a control test. Latest row per control is the primary evidence when judging control effectiveness.
Issue table "grc__Issue__c": Findings and remediation issues raised against a business unit, tracked by status until closed. Open issues inform inherent risk scoring for that unit.
